title: Emotions & Opinions
shortSummary: Say how you feel and share your opinion, the language of real conversation.
culturalNote: Germans have a reputation for honesty and directness: if you ask for an opinion, you will usually get a frank one, and this is meant as respect, not rudeness. Sharing a clear, well-reasoned view is valued in conversation. The phrase 'Ich finde...' (I think / I find...) is the everyday way to open an opinion without sounding too formal.

keyPhrases.english: I'm glad / looking forward to it
What do you think of that?

grammar.explanation: Many feeling verbs are reflexive, using a pronoun like mich (myself): 'Ich freue mich' (I'm glad), 'Ich ärgere mich' (I'm annoyed). The reflexive pronoun matches the subject.

examples.de: Ich freue mich auf das Wochenende.
examples.en: I'm looking forward to the weekend.

grammar.commonMistake: Dropping the reflexive pronoun: it is 'Ich freue mich', not 'Ich freue'.

grammar.rule: dass sends the verb to the end
grammar.explanation: When you give an opinion with 'Ich finde, dass...' or 'Ich glaube, dass...', the verb of the second clause moves to the very end: 'Ich finde, dass der Film gut ist'.

examples.de: Ich glaube, dass es richtig ist.
examples.en: I think that it is right.

examples.de: Ich finde, dass du Recht hast.
examples.en: I think that you are right.

grammar.commonMistake: Keeping normal word order after 'dass': the verb must go to the end.

dialogue.setup: Two friends, Mara and Tim, leave the cinema and share their opinions about the film.

lines.speaker: Mara
lines.english: What do you think of the film?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.german: Ich finde, dass er sehr gut war. Und du?
lines.english: I think it was very good. And you?

lines.speaker: Mara
lines.german: Das sehe ich anders. Mir war er zu lang.
lines.english: I see it differently. It was too long for me.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.german: Wirklich? Die Musik hat mir aber gefallen.
lines.english: Really? But I liked the music.

lines.speaker: Mara
lines.german: Da stimme ich dir zu. Die Musik war toll.
lines.english: I agree with you there. The music was great.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.german: Freust du dich auf den zweiten Teil?
lines.english: Are you looking forward to the second part?

quiz.question: How do you say 'I agree'?
quiz.explanation: zustimmen is separable: stimme second, zu at the end.

quiz.question: Which phrase opens an opinion?
quiz.explanation: Ich finde, dass... is the everyday way to give an opinion.

quiz.question: Which sentence has correct word order?
options: Ich finde, dass der Film ist gut
Ich finde, dass der Film gut ist
Ich finde, der Film dass gut ist
Ich finde gut dass der Film ist
quiz.correctAnswer: Ich finde, dass der Film gut ist
quiz.explanation: After 'dass', the verb 'ist' goes to the very end.

quiz.question: Arrange: das / du / findest / wie
quiz.explanation: Question word wie, verb findest second, then du and das.
